title: A Ballad Apout de Rowdies
content:
В В В В De moon shines ofer de cloudlens,
В В В В Und de cloudts plow ofer de sea,
В В В В Und I vent to Coney Island,
В В В В Und I took mein Schatz mit me.
В В В В Mein Schatz, Katrina Bauer,
В В В В I gife her mein heart und vortdt;
В В В В Boot ve tidnпїЅt know vot beoples
В В В В De Dampfsschiff hafe cot on poard.

В В В В De preeze plowed cool und bleasant,
В В В В We looket at de town
В В В В Mit sonn-light on de shdeebles,
В В В В Und wetter fanes doorninпїЅ round.
В В В В Ve sat on de deck in a gorner
В В В В Und dropled nopody dere,
В В В В Vhen all aroundt oos de rowdies
В В В В Peginned to plackguard und schvear.

В В В В A voman mit a papy
В В В В Vos sittinпїЅ in de blace;
В В В В Von tooket a chew tobacco
В В В В Und trowed it indo her vace.
В В В В De voman got coonvulshons,
В В В В De papy pegin to gry;
В В В В Und de rowdies shkreemed out a laffin,
В В В В Und saidt dat de fun was пїЅhigh.пїЅ

В В В В Pimepy ve become some hoonger,
В В В В Katrina Bauer und I,
В В В В I openet de lit of mine pasket,
В В В В Und pringed out a cherry bie.
В В В В A cherry kooken mit pretzels,
В В В В пїЅHow goot!пїЅ Katrina said,
В В В В Vhen a rowdy snatched it from her,
В В В В Und preaked it ofer mine het.

В В В В I dells him he pe a plackguart,
В В В В I gifed him a biece my mind,
В В В В I vouldt saidt it pefore a tousand,
В В В В Mit der teufel himself pehind.
В В В В Den he knocks me down mit a sloong-shot,
В В В В Und peats me plack and plue;
В В В В Und de plackguards kick me,
В В В В Dill I vainted, und dat ish drue.

В В В В De rich American beoples
В В В В DonпїЅt know how de rowdies shtrike
В В В В Der poor hardtworkinпїЅ Sharman,
В В В В He knows it more ash he like.
В В В В If de Deutsche speakers und bapers
В В В В Are somedimes too hard on dis land,
В В В В Shoost dink how de Deutsch kit driven
В В В В Along by de rowdyпїЅs hand!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Charles G. Leland represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Charles G. Leland's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
